name: MotherDuck API
what_it_does: MotherDuck managed cloud DuckDB — run DuckDB analytical queries in the cloud with shared databases, collaboration, and hybrid local+cloud execution via the DuckDB client.
use_cases: Sharing DuckDB analytical databases between multiple agents or team members without file transfer
Hybrid local+cloud query execution — agents run queries locally but sync results to MotherDuck
Serverless analytical queries without managing compute — MotherDuck handles scaling
Storing agent-generated analytical datasets in the cloud with full SQL access
Collaborative data analysis — multiple agents querying the same cloud DuckDB database concurrently
not_for: OLTP workloads — MotherDuck inherits DuckDB's single-writer limitation
Real-time streaming ingestion — not designed for high-frequency write workloads
Teams not already using DuckDB — the value is cloud-extending DuckDB, not a standalone warehouse
best_when: Your agent uses DuckDB locally and needs cloud sharing, collaboration, or persistence without moving to a full data warehouse.
avoid_when: You need a general-purpose cloud database — use PostgreSQL or Snowflake; MotherDuck is DuckDB-specific.

auth.notes: MotherDuck access token passed in DuckDB connection string. Token scoped to your MotherDuck account. Shared databases accessible to invited team members.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 10GB storage; 25 compute credits/month free
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro: $25/month includes compute credits; pay per compute credit beyond that
pricing.notes: Free tier is useful for exploration. Compute credits charged for cloud query execution. Local DuckDB queries with MotherDuck connection don't consume compute credits.

performance.rate_limits: Based on compute credits; no hard request rate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: DuckDB error messages are excellent — specific and actionable. MotherDuck connection errors include guidance on token and database setup. MCP server available for natural language SQL queries.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SQL SELECT queries are fully idempotent. Writes with IF NOT EXISTS or REPLACE patterns are safe. DuckDB transaction support for complex multi-step writes.
known_agent_gotchas: Token authentication happens in DuckDB connection string — must use md: prefix in database name
Shared databases require explicit share invitation — agents can't access other users' databases without grant
MotherDuck queries route to cloud compute — network latency adds to DuckDB's normally sub-ms local latency
DuckDB version pinning is important — MotherDuck may lag behind latest DuckDB release
MCP server requires running DuckDB locally — not a pure cloud API like BigQuery or Snowflake

security_score_components.security_notes: Token authentication. Database-level sharing permissions. SOC2 in progress. All data encrypted in transit and at rest. DuckDB is MIT licensed — auditable core.
